title: [heliolisk] Battle Guide
summary: A sun-reliant special sweeper that turns 109 SpA and 109 Speed into a 160+ effective damage output through [solar-power].
strengths: [solar-power] raises effective SpA into the 160s under sun
Base 109 Speed beats nearly every unboosted [timid]-nature threat
[weather-ball] becomes a Fire attack in sun for Grass and Steel coverage
weaknesses: Fighting and [ground] both hit for 2x; close-combat or earthquake outright OHKOs
62 HP and 52 Def means even neutral hits can finish the job
Loses its SpA modifier the moment Rain, Sand, or Snow overwrites sun

recommendedMoves.reason: The reliable [electric] STAB main that punishes Water and Flying cores
[normal] STAB that ignores substitute and disguise walls

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[heliolisk] is the [electric]/[normal] frill-lizard built entirely around hidden ability [solar-power]. Base 109 SpA and 109 Speed is a clean symmetric statline, and under sun the SpA modifier lifts damage output into the neighborhood of a genuine wallbreaker. The whole pitch of the card is that it trades bulk and longevity for one of the cleanest [electric]/[normal] offensive angles in the format.

## Strengths
Under sun, [thunderbolt] rolls through Water and Flying cores, and [hyper-voice] provides a second STAB that ignores both substitute and disguise. Base 109 Speed with a [timid] nature clears 357 actual Speed, outrunning every unboosted offensive threat that sits in the 100-108 bracket. [weather-ball] is the real trick — in sun it becomes a Fire-type move, meaning [heliolisk] gets Grass, Steel, Ice, and Bug coverage off a single slot without a dedicated Fire move. [focus-blast] rounds the set out with an answer to defensive Rock and Steel pivots.

## Weaknesses & Answers
Fighting and [ground] are both neutral-to-devastating matchups. 62 HP and 52 Def give [heliolisk] no room to absorb anything, so any close-combat or earthquake that lands ends the sequence. [solar-power] ticks 1/8 HP every turn, which means the sun timer is effectively a death clock. Priority — extreme-speed, ice-shard, aqua-jet — is also structurally lethal once any chip damage accumulates. A mirror weather setter (opposing Rain, Sand, or Snow) erases the SpA modifier and leaves [heliolisk] as a brittle mid-tier special attacker.

## Recommended Moveset
[thunderbolt] is the always-on STAB main. [hyper-voice] is picked as the second STAB specifically to break subs and disguise, because otherwise stall cores can cheese the [electric]-only coverage. [weather-ball] holds the coverage slot because its sun-Fire conversion handles Grass, Steel, Bug, and Ice off one button, letting the fourth slot stay flexible. [focus-blast] takes that fourth slot, accepting 70% accuracy because it's the only thing on the learnset that actually hurts physically defensive Rock or Steel switch-ins. If the team can't afford accuracy rolls, dark-pulse is the safer swap.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[focus-sash] is the default item. It guarantees one priority or quick-claw hit survived, which is typically the only window [heliolisk] needs. [choice-scarf] is the alternative when sun uptime is unreliable and the team still needs a Speed-tier anchor; it also lets [heliolisk] revenge scarfed threats. The ability must be the hidden [solar-power] — dry-skin is an entirely different defensive archetype. Tera [electric] pushes [thunderbolt] into one-shot territory, and Tera [ground] flips the [ground] weakness while giving [heliolisk] a mirror against opposing Electrics.

## Teammates & Synergy
A dedicated sun setter (Ninetales being the cleanest pick) is mandatory; [heliolisk] has no ability-based weather of its own. The team also needs a [ground] immunity pivot to cover earthquake switches, and a priority user or Fighting-resist pivot to cover the Fighting matchup. A stealth-rock lead multiplies [heliolisk]'s effective damage by pushing 2HKOs into OHKOs and takes pressure off the [focus-blast] accuracy rolls.

## Play Patterns
Lead the sun setter on turn one, bring [heliolisk] in on turn two behind a slow pivot or a free KO. Open with [thunderbolt] into anything neutral; save [weather-ball] for the obvious Grass or Steel switch-in; only click [focus-blast] when the accuracy roll won't lose the game. Tera early and aggressively — the moment a close-combat or earthquake is telegraphed, flipping the matchup is more valuable than holding the Tera for a mirror. [heliolisk]'s sequence is short and sharp: two turns of damage, then either a KO or a swap.
